page_title: Visitor record: How to apply - Canada.ca

h3: Step 1: Make sure you have what you need
Step 3: Prepare your answers for the online tool
Step 4: Create your online account or sign in

sections.heading: Applying online
items: When you apply online, we get your application instantly, which can help us process your application faster. If you already applied on paper, you don’t have to resubmit your application online.

sections.heading: Step 1: Make sure you have what you need
bullets: a scanner or camera to create electronic copies of your documents
a valid credit or debit card

sections.heading: Step 2: Read the instruction guide
items: Even if you apply online, you should read the instruction guide before you complete your application. The guide will explain how to complete each field on the form.

sections.heading: Step 3: Prepare your answers for the online tool
items: Before you can upload your forms, you must answer some questions. We use your answers to create a personalized document checklist for you. To apply for a visitor record, you must provide these answers in the online tool:
bullets: For the first question, “What would you like to do in Canada?”, select “Visit.”
When asked, “What is your current country/territory of residence?”, select “Canada.”

sections.heading: Step 4: Create your online account or sign in
items: You need an account to apply online. You can use your account to

sections.heading: If you can’t apply online
items: You can submit a paper application in any of these situations:
What to do if you can’t apply online
bullets: You can’t apply online because of a disability.
There’s a problem with the online application.
